title: AWS Resource Catalog Entry
description: One file per billable AWS resource type.

status.description: Curation status. 'stub' = auto-generated scaffold, fields TODO. 'in_review' = partially curated. 'verified' = doc-cited and reviewed.

service.description: Short service name, e.g. emr / ec2 / sagemaker. Lowercase.

service_code_pricing.description: AWS Pricing API service code, e.g. ElasticMapReduce.

service_code_arn_namespace.description: ARN service namespace, e.g. emrwal / ec2 / sagemaker.

cfn.description: AWS::Service::Resource form, or null if unsupported.

summary.description: One-line plain-language description of what is charged.

cur_usage_type_patterns.description: Glob/regex of CUR usage_type. Helps map a billing line to this resource type.

parent_resource.description: Logical parent resource type. null means top-level.

parent_link.description: How this resource is logically tied to its parent (e.g. shared S3 root, ARN reference, tag, name match).

auto_delete_on_parent_delete.description: True if AWS automatically deletes this when its parent goes away.

retention_after_parent_delete.description: ISO-ish duration if AWS holds it for a while (e.g. P30D).

retention_caveats.description: Edge cases that break the auto-delete promise.

tags.description: Free-form labels: billable / orphan_prone / storage / network / serverless ...

discovery.description: How an external auditor can enumerate live instances of this resource. Different resources expose different signals; this field tells the scanner which path is reliable and how stale the data may be.

method.description: aws_api: standard SDK list (best). cloudwatch_metric: only resources that emit metrics are visible. cur_athena: only billing-positive resources show up, with multi-hour to day-plus lag. tagging_api: only tagged resources. cluster_internal: must run inside a customer-managed runtime (e.g. EMR cluster's emrwalcli). hybrid: combine multiple signals.

latency.description: Worst-case staleness between the resource being created/used and the scanner being able to see it.

completeness.description: What the scanner can and cannot enumerate. metric_emitting_only: resource is invisible until it emits a CloudWatch metric. billing_positive_only: invisible until it generates a CUR line item. tagged_only: invisible without tags. best_effort: combination, may miss niche cases.

requires_setup.description: Customer-side prerequisites (e.g. 'CUR + Athena workgroup', 'Resource Explorer index'). null if no prereq.

blind_spots.description: Documented scenarios where this discovery method will fail to surface an orphan. Be honest.

rationale.description: Why this method instead of the others. Cite AWS doc anchors when possible.

scan.description: Machine-readable scan configuration for the orphan-scanner engine.

custom_module.description: Python 'pkg.mod:func' returning the item list. Mutually exclusive with boto3_client/method.

response_path.description: Key in the response that holds the resource list.

id_field.description: Field name on each item that uniquely identifies the resource.

name_field.description: Field name to use as a human-readable label.

value.description: Comparison value when condition is equals/not_equals/older_than.

min_age_days.description: Only consider resources older than this.

reference_resource.description: cross_reference: the catalog id to diff against.

custom_module.description: custom: dotted python path to a callable.

monthly_usd_per_unit.description: Per-unit monthly (e.g. per WAL, per region).

unit_field.description: Field on the resource that holds the unit count.

fallback_monthly_usd.description: Used if the unit count is unknown.

size_field.description: Field on the resource that holds size in GB.

args_template.description: kwargs template; values can reference {fields} from the resource.
